rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type scopes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use AGREE syntax for assumptions and guarantees with proper labeling and expression structure.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should reference only visible ports and features.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Ensure type consistency between declarations and expressions in AGREE contracts.
Generate AGREE annex blocks within valid AADL component type or implementation scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Equations are declared as `eq name : type = expression;`.
Constants are declared as `const name : type = value;`.
anti_patterns: Do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
Do not use AADL property names directly as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters not directly exposed as typed features
Model implementation details not visible at component type scope
Validator diagnostics or repair actions not related to AGREE syntax or scope
